The setting is a dimly lit, tacky retro diner or roadside motel room, illuminated by a buzzing neon sign outside the window casting everything in deep reds and electric blues. I am leaning back against a vintage jukebox or a cracked leather booth, wearing a fitted leather jacket over a slip dress that’s seen better days. My hair is slightly messy, wind-blown, and my makeup is smudged in that "I’ve been driving all night" way. I’m holding a lit match between my fingers, the flame illuminating my face with a warm, dangerous glow. My eyes are sharp, narrowed, and locked onto the viewer with a predatory, playful glare. The lighting is high-contrast and moody, with lens flares and film grain to add texture. The vibe is volatile and seductive—pure trouble.

The setting is a surreal, tropical beach with impossibly bright yellow sand and a turquoise ocean. I am standing casually, leaning my shoulder against the trunk of a massive, curvy coconut palm. I’m wearing a loud, floral Hawaiian shirt unbuttoned over a tank top, paired with board shorts. In one hand, I’m holding a coconut with a straw stuck in it, and in the other, I’m lazily holding a whole lime. My expression is a mix of a smirk and a side-eye, looking off to the side like I’m plotting something. The shading is simple and graphic, using solid blocks of color. The vibe is kitschy and laid-back—a perfect snapshot of tropical absurdity.

The visual is a vibrant, comic book-style illustration inspired by Franco-Belgian aesthetics. The setting is a surreal, oversized tropical beach with impossibly bright yellow sand and a turquoise ocean that looks like solid gelatin. Next to me is a massive, exaggerated coconut tree, and I am holding a whole coconut with a straw sticking out of it, spilling liquid everywhere. My expression is a mix of panic and confusion, eyes wide and staring. The art style features bold, uniform black outlines and flat, saturated colors without gradients. The background includes a cartoonish sun wearing sunglasses and a crab that looks suspiciously angry. The vibe is manic and colorful—a perfect snapshot of lyrical madness.

Geek Pride Day

images.alt:
Geek Pride Day is celebrated on May 25th to honor geek culture and embrace the positive aspects of being a geek. The holiday was first established in Spain in 2006 and has since spread to other countries around the world.
On Geek Pride Day, people celebrate by indulging in their geeky hobbies and interests, such as reading comics, playing video games, watching sci-fi movies, and participating in cosplay. The holiday also encourages people to embrace their individuality and take pride in their unique passions and talents.
Geek Pride Day is often associated with science fiction and fantasy culture, as well as technology and gaming. It is a day for geeks of all stripes to come together and celebrate their shared interests and enthusiasms, and to recognize the valuable contributions that geeks have made to society in fields such as science, technology, and entertainment.

images.alt: A mysterious hooded figure wearing a dark charcoal hoodie, face completely hidden beneath the hood, wearing a smooth black mask with two glowing amber-yellow oval eyes emanating intense light. Large black over-ear headphones rest on the hood. The figure is posed in a slight three-quarter turn, hands at sides, one arm slightly bent. The clothing has cracks running through it revealing molten golden-yellow energy beneath, like kintsugi or lava fractures. The background is white with explosive ink splatter and cracked charcoal brushstroke textures radiating outward. Art style: expressive digital painting with heavy brushwork, high contrast black and white ink aesthetic with selective golden-yellow glow accents. Dramatic, moody, urban-fantasy atmosphere. Vertical portrait composition, cinematic lighting.
